title: FALSIFIED: HLA-A monocyte methylation does NOT track distance-to-nearest-ERV3-16A3_I-int beyond what a random reference point achieves

subject.display: FALSIFIED: HLA-A monocyte methylation does NOT track distance-to-nearest-ERV3-16A3_I-int beyond what a random reference point achieves
subject.prose: Migrated from v1.1 as a literal subject because the claim was marked withdrawn/falsified at migration time. Re-author with a typed subject when resuming.

structured.note: literal wrap chosen due to withdrawn/falsified status

context.free_form: migrated from v1.1 without domain-specific context

source.note: Monocyte WGBS methylation per CpG in the HLA-A coordinate window (chr6:29,941,000-29,946,000).
Per-locus genomic coordinates of ERV3-16A3_I-int instances near HLA-A.
Single random reference position at chr6:29,942,824 used as Phase 5 Test 3 negative control. Selected once, seeded; not cherry-picked.

statistics.name: abs_spearman_rho_erv_anchor
statistics.note: Distance-to-nearest-ERV3-16A3_I-int anchor; was the original Phase 2 finding.

statistics.name: abs_spearman_rho_random_anchor
statistics.note: Single random reference anchor at chr6:29,942,824. Phase 5 Test 3 finding: random anchor outperforms ERV anchor on the same window.

inference.justification: Original (Phase 2) hypothesis: HLA-A methylation tracks ERV-start distance because HERV silencing 'shadows' nearby HLA. Phase 5 Test 3 falsifier: if a single random reference anchor in the same window produces a stronger |rho| than the ERV anchor, the spatial autocorrelation in the methylation track explains the apparent ERV-anchor signal. The inference licenses the original framing only if rho_erv > rho_random.
inference.failure_mode: Falsified iff rho_erv <= rho_random. Observed: 0.68 vs 0.95 — random outperforms ERV. The Phase 5 Test 3 falsifier triggered. The HLA-A ERV-methylation-shadow framing is revoked.

conclusion.assertion: The Phase 2 finding that HLA-A monocyte methylation correlates with distance to ERV3-16A3_I-int does NOT survive the Phase 5 Test 3 negative control: a single random reference point in the same window produces a stronger absolute Spearman rho (0.95) than the ERV anchor (0.68). The 'ERV methylation shadow' mechanism is FALSIFIED at this scale; the Phase 2 signal is attributable to spatial autocorrelation in the methylation track, not ERV proximity per se.

scope.scale_note: Falsifier is at the single-anchor / single-cell-type scale tested in Phase 5. Other ERV families and other cell types may yield different verdicts; this claim object retires the specific HLA-A x ERV3-16A3_I-int x monocyte mechanism only.

confidence.note: Single-anchor comparison; multi-anchor / permutation-based formalization is queued as a sibling claim.

external_assumptions.statement: A single random reference point is a fair negative control for spatial-autocorrelation contamination of distance-based correlations within a small genomic window.
The HLA-A monocyte WGBS data quality is sufficient that a 0.27-magnitude gap in |rho| (0.95 vs 0.68) is well outside measurement noise.

This claim supersedes the Phase 2 'ERV methylation shadow' framing previously circulated internally. The supersession edge is recorded in HLA experiment/claims/README.md (claim 5) and in HLA experiment/README.md § Supersession is load-bearing; in v1.1 the schema does not yet have first-class supersedes/superseded_by fields, so this assumption documents the relationship until v1.2.
